reasoning: The MLS playoff format is very forgiving, with 9 out of 15 teams in each conference making the postseason (a 60% baseline rate for any given team). Historically, MLS expansion teams have had mixed results in their inaugural seasons, making the playoffs around 45-50% of the time across the league's modern era. However, the recently expanded 9-team playoff format makes qualifying significantly easier than in the past. San Diego FC benefits from well-funded ownership, the structural foundation of the 'Right to Dream' academy network, and serious ambition, highlighted by the marquee Designated Player signing of Hirving 'Chucky' Lozano. While expansion teams predictably face challenges with roster continuity, chemistry, and depth, the forgiving playoff threshold combined with their strong proactive team-building puts SDFC's chances close to a coin flip. I slightly discount the league average (60%) to account for expansion pains, arriving at 52%.
